items.title: US Dollar Index rebounds to 99.00 as oil price recovery prompts hawkish Fed risks
items.summary: The US Dollar Index (DXY), which gauges the Greenback’s value against six major currencies, trades 0.25% higher to near 99.00 during the European trading session on Monday.

items.title: WTI Crude Oil stalls below $98.00 with Iran peace hopes still alive
items.summary: Oil prices jumped on Monday following the failure of the peace talks between the US and Iran and the US pledge to block the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: HUF: Election outcome boosts Forint as EU risk recedes – Danske Bank
items.summary: Danske Research Team reports that Hungary’s election delivered a decisive victory for Péter Magyar’s pro‑EU Tisza party over Viktor Orban’s Fidesz, easing concerns about future EU budget and Ukraine aid negotiations.

items.title: Iran criticizes US for blockade of vessels
items.summary: According to a report from the Iranian state media Islamic Republic of Iran Broadcasting (IRIB), a spokesperson from Iran’s Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters said during European trading hours on Monday that Tehran condemns the United States (US) intentions to blockade vessels entering and exiti

items.title: S&P 500: Earnings optimism versus conflict shock – Deutsche Bank
items.summary: Deutsche Bank’s strategists highlight that S&P 500 futures are lower as the Iran conflict escalates and energy prices jump, pressuring global risk sentiment.

items.title: Australian Dollar trades mixed on renewed Middle East conflicts, job data awaited
items.summary: The Australian Dollar (AUD) demonstrates a mixed performance against its major currency peers, still trading 0.2% lower to near 0.7050 against the US Dollar (USD) during the European trading session on Monday, even after recovering a major chunk of its opening losses.

items.title: Canadian Dollar pares losses vs USD as rallying oil prices counter hawkish Fed bets
items.summary: The USD/CAD pair retreats a few pips from the Asian session high and currently trades around the 1.3860-1.3855 region, up around 0.15% for the day.

items.title: WTI Price Forecast: Rallies to $98 as Trump aims to blockade Hormuz
items.summary: West Texas Intermediate (WTI), futures on NYMEX, trade 7.6% higher to near $98.00 during the Asian trading session on Monday.

items.title: PBOC sets USD/CNY reference rate at 6.8657 vs. 6.8654 previous
items.summary: On Monday, the People’s Bank of China (PBOC) sets the USD/CNY central rate for the trading session ahead at 6.8657 compared to Friday's of 6.8654 and 6.8395 Reuters estimate.

items.title: NZD/USD recovers slightly from daily low, keeps the red above 0.5800 on firmer USD
items.summary: The NZD/USD pair opens with a bearish gap at the start of a new trading week in reaction to failed US-Iran peace talks over the weekend and trades around the 0.5800 mark during the Asian session.

items.title: Canadian Dollar falls further as USD haven demand counters WTI surge
items.summary: The Canadian Dollar (CAD) is extending its pullback from two-week highs of 1.3844 against the US Dollar (USD) early Monday, with USD/CAD capitalizing on the renewed demand for the Greenback as a go-to safe-haven.

items.title: WTI jumps roughly 8% toward $100 as US blockades Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: West Texas Intermediate (WTI) – the US oil benchmark – has opened the week with a bullish gap, climbing roughly 8%, looking to retarget the $100 threshold.
